skill.name: product-launch-video
skill.description: Creates product launch videos from a product URL, pasted script, or launch brief. It guides capture, storyboard planning, visual direction, audio, captions, frame assembly, validation, and final MP4 rendering for promotional product videos.
skill.summary: Create launch-ready product videos from URLs, scripts, and marketing briefs.
skill.author: heygen-com

security_audit.summary: Static analysis found many pattern matches. Most weak-crypto, backtick, and traversal alerts in Markdown and regex-heavy parsing code are false positives. However, the skill intentionally runs external commands, uses network capture and CDN assets, reads environment and hidden credentials, and writes generated project files across user-selected paths, so it should not be published without maintainer review and sandboxing.

high_findings.title: External Command Execution Across the Workflow
high_findings.description: The skill instructs the agent to run HyperFrames CLI commands and includes Node scripts that spawn other processes such as the shared audio engine, ffprobe, and ffmpeg. This is expected for video generation, but it gives the skill significant execution capability on the host system.
high_findings.confidence_reasoning: Direct process spawning is present and semantically required by the workflow. I did not find evidence of malicious command construction, but the execution surface is real.

high_findings.title: Credential and Environment Access in Media Pipeline
high_findings.description: The workflow references sign-in state, API key availability, a hidden HeyGen credential, and an environment override for the media engine path. These are legitimate integration points, but they increase the impact if the skill or its dependencies are altered.
high_findings.confidence_reasoning: The references to environment keys, hidden credentials, and executable engine override are explicit. The code does not show exfiltration, so the concern is elevated integration risk rather than confirmed abuse.

high_findings.title: Broad Filesystem Writes from User-Selected Project Paths
high_findings.description: Several scripts resolve CLI-provided project paths and write or copy generated files such as frame.md, caption skins, captions, and staged assets. This is normal for a generator, but it can overwrite files if run with an unsafe project path.
high_findings.confidence_reasoning: The filesystem writes are direct and use resolved paths from command-line arguments. The intended workflow constrains them to a project directory, but there is no hard sandbox in these scripts.

medium_findings.title: Remote CDN Script Loaded in Generated Output
medium_findings.description: Generated HTML includes GSAP from jsDelivr. This is a common frontend dependency pattern, but it adds a network supply-chain dependency to rendered compositions.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The hardcoded CDN URL is explicit. The risk is dependency and network exposure, not evidence of malicious code in this repository.

medium_findings.title: Static Scanner Heuristics Mostly Match Benign Text and Regex
medium_findings.description: Many alerts for weak cryptography, Ruby backticks, C2 keywords, and path traversal occur in Markdown guidance, examples, regex literals, or comments. I did not find semantic evidence that these matches implement malicious behavior.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: Targeted review showed prose examples and parser regex usage, not cryptographic code or C2 behavior. Because the repository is large, this is a representative dismissal of repeated heuristic matches.

low_findings.title: No Prompt Injection Attempt Found in Reviewed Files
low_findings.description: A targeted search for override language, fake system messages, pre-approval claims, and instructions to skip security review found no evidence of prompt injection attempts.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The reviewed instruction files contain normal workflow and worker guidance. No matching injection phrases were found, but this does not prove every line is risk-free.

dangerous_patterns.title: Process Spawning
dangerous_patterns.description: Scripts use spawnSync to invoke Node and media binaries during audio and assembly tasks.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The process-spawn APIs and executable names are directly visible in the code.

dangerous_patterns.title: Hidden Credential and API Key Use
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill relies on sign-in state, optional API keys, and the HeyGen credential for media retrieval.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The credential and key references are explicit in the workflow text. No credential exfiltration code was found.

dangerous_patterns.title: Generated File Writes
dangerous_patterns.description: The scripts write project outputs, captions, copied skins, and staged media assets under resolved project paths.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The write and copy calls are direct. They are part of expected generator behavior but still require filesystem trust.

content.value_statement: Product teams need polished launch videos without rebuilding the production workflow each time. This skill turns a URL, script, or brief into a structured HyperFrames video with storyboard, audio, captions, frames, and render steps.
actual_capabilities: Routes product launch requests and rejects unrelated video intents.
Captures product URLs and stores brand text, tokens, and asset descriptions.
Creates storyboard and script files for frame-by-frame launch narratives.
Builds a brand-adapted frame design system from shipped presets.
Generates audio metadata, captions, transitions, and assembled HyperFrames output.
Guides validation, preview, and final MP4 rendering after user approval.
limitations: Requires HyperFrames tooling and related sibling skills to be installed.
Uses external commands, filesystem writes, and optional HeyGen credentials.
Not intended for general explainers, pull requests, or captioning existing videos.
Human approval is required at storyboard and final render stages.

use_cases.title: Launch a SaaS Feature
use_cases.description: Turn a feature page into a short launch video with brand-aware visuals, narration, captions, and a clear final render path.

use_cases.title: Promote a New App
use_cases.description: Use a launch brief and product URL to create a structured product reveal video for website, email, or social distribution.

use_cases.title: Standardize Video Production
use_cases.description: Apply a repeatable workflow for capture, storyboard approval, frame production, validation, preview, and MP4 rendering.

prompt_templates.title: Start From a URL
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a product launch video for this URL: [product URL]. Use a concise story for [target audience] and make it [length] seconds.

prompt_templates.title: Use My Script
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a product launch video from this script. Keep the message focused on [main benefit], and ask before changing the wording.

prompt_templates.title: Build a Feature Reveal
prompt_templates.prompt: Make a launch video for [feature name]. Show the problem, the product moment, the proof, and a clear final call to action.

prompt_templates.title: Produce a Full Campaign Cut
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a polished launch video for [product]. Use [URL or brief], match the brand, include narration and captions, validate the result, and prepare the MP4 render after approval.

output_examples.input: A URL for a SaaS analytics product and a request for a 30 second launch video.
output_examples.output: A storyboard with approved frames, a script, brand-adapted visual direction, generated captions, assembled preview files, and a render-ready MP4 path.

output_examples.input: A pasted product announcement script for a mobile app launch.
output_examples.output: A structured video plan that preserves or restructures the script by user choice, then maps each beat to visual frames and audio timing.

output_examples.input: A short brief for a feature reveal with no website assets.
output_examples.output: A no-capture project setup with manual brand tokens, placeholder asset notes, storyboard frames, and production steps for a silent or narrated reveal.

best_practices: Use a clear product URL or concise launch brief before starting capture.
Approve the storyboard before frame production begins.
Run the final validation steps before rendering the MP4.
anti_patterns: Do not use this for non-product explainers or generic website tours.
Do not skip sign-in and credential decisions when media services are needed.
Do not render before reviewing the assembled preview.

faq.question: What inputs does this skill accept?
faq.answer: It accepts a product URL, a pasted launch script, or a written marketing brief.

faq.question: Can it create the final video file?
faq.answer: Yes. It guides the HyperFrames render step after validation and user approval.

faq.question: Does it need HeyGen credentials?
faq.answer: Credentials are optional for some media features, but the workflow checks sign-in status before continuing.

faq.question: Can I use it for a general explainer?
faq.answer: No. It is designed for product launch, promotion, and reveal videos.

faq.question: Does it support captions?
faq.answer: Yes. It can build caption groups and caption HTML from audio timing metadata.

faq.question: Why is the security risk high?
faq.answer: It runs external commands, writes project files, uses network resources, and can use environment or hidden credentials.
